news.summary: Hg has boosted its ownership in the enterprise software provider IFS and now holds a significant stake, making it a co-control shareholder alongside EQT. The existing minority shareholder, TA Associates, continues to have an investment in the company. As part of this transaction, a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority has also become a new investor.
news.headline: EQT, Hg and TA's IFS valued at EUR 15B in minority stake sale; Hg becomes co-control shareholder with EQT, alongside investors ADIA and CPP Investments.

news.summary: A venture capital firm from Fort Worth, Texas has decided to support 16 promising companies that are experiencing rapid growth and making a tangible impact in the real world. So far, these companies have collectively served 149 victims and led to the arrest of 39 traffickers.
news.headline: Eagle Freedom Fund raises $18 million for tech solutions against human trafficking, backing 16 companies with 149 victims served, 39 traffickers arrested.

news.summary: The European Central Bank (ECB) is expressing concerns over the rising popularity of US dollar-pegged stablecoins in Europe. ECB executive Piero Cipollone advocates for a digital euro as a strategic response to combat the dominance of dollar-backed stablecoins. Cipollone emphasizes that launching a central bank digital currency (CBDC) can help preserve the eurozone's monetary sovereignty and limit the influence of foreign currency stablecoins. He warns that the increasing crypto-friendly stance of the US could lead to further strengthening of the dollar's role in cross-border payments. The ECB aims to promote cash usage but acknowledges the limitations of cash in online transactions which are becoming more prevalent in European commerce. Cipollone urges prompt action in developing regulations for the digital euro and reaffirms the importance of cash as a unique sovereign payment method. The proposed digital euro has faced criticism and skepticism, particularly regarding data privacy concerns, as European consumers appear hesitant to adopt the potential CBDC.
news.headline: ECB urges digital euro to counter US dollar stablecoins in Europe, seeks to limit foreign influence. Source : Bloomberg.

news.summary: Join Church of the Highlands in celebrating Easter with hope, joy, and new beginnings at one of its 26 campuses in Alabama and Georgia. The festivities begin on Good Friday, April 11th, and continue through Easter Sunday, April 13th. This year, the church anticipates a record-breaking turnout, with over 10,000 attendees expected to participate in the various services and events. Come and join the community in commemorating the resurrection of Jesus Christ and experiencing a weekend filled with spiritual growth and fellowship.

news.summary: Hg has raised its ownership in the enterprise software provider IFS, now holding a significant stake alongside EQT, making them co-control shareholders. During this transaction, existing minority shareholder TA Associates maintained their investment. Among the new investors involved in this deal is a subsidiary of the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority.

news.summary: The merger of the two companies will unite a total of 2,250 workers, establishing it as a major player in the energy retail sector in North America. With an impressive reach, the combined entity will cater to approximately 7.5 million RCEs (residential customer equivalents) each year. Just Energy will maintain its autonomy and operate as usual under its current brands. This collaboration signifies a significant milestone in the industry, showcasing the powerful impact of this joint venture.

news.summary: In Delray Beach, Florida, recent data suggests that the worldwide US Medical Aesthetics Market is forecasted to surge to approximately US$16.38 billion by 2030, up from US$7.62 billion in 2024, representing a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 13.6%. This increase is largely driven by the rising demand within the aging population for anti-aging solutions.
news.headline: US Medical Aesthetics Market to reach $16.38B by 2030 from $7.62B in 2024, at 13.6% CAGR, driven by aging population.

news.summary: A resident from Lansing, Michigan innovated a new product called BENNY'S SLIDE GUIDE aimed at improving the process of disassembling and reassembling small handguns by securely holding the slide in place. The invention, introduced on April 9, 2025, in Pittsburgh, addresses the inventor's desire for a more efficient way to handle the handgun slide. This innovative tool is set to revolutionize weapon maintenance procedures, promising increased ease and accuracy for handling small firearms.
news.headline: Inventor creates BENNY'S SLIDE GUIDE for small firearms; aims to improve disassembly process; named KSG-122.

news.summary: Dublin - ResearchAndMarkets.com has recently added the "Electric Three-wheeler Market, India, FY2024-FY2030" report to its offerings. In India, the electric three-wheeler (E3W) industry has become a key player in the nation's move towards sustainable transportation. With factors such as rapid urbanization, government support, and rising environmental awareness driving its growth, the sector is anticipated to continue thriving. E3Ws, known for being affordable and eco-friendly, are gaining popularity and are being utilized more extensively.

news.summary: Orlando-based Travel + Leisure Co. news.summary: Sierra Vista, Arizona-based Rite Way Heating, Cooling and Plumbing LLC has recently acquired the residential HVAC division of Berg Enterprises, Inc. Established by Dick Berg in 1974, Berg's Heating & Air Conditioning has been a respected provider of residential HVAC services.
news.headline: Rite Way Heating, Cooling and Plumbing buys Berg's Heating & Air; Berg's has 50-year legacy in Cochise County.

news.summary: In March, Dwight Capital and its affiliate REIT, Dwight Mortgage Trust (“DMT”), completed real estate financings totaling more than $364 million. Notable transactions included a bridge loan for Silverwoods in New Jersey, a bridge loan for a three-property skilled nursing portfolio in Rhode Island, and a HUD 223(f) refinance for Skyview Heights in Washington State. DMT finalized a $50 million bridge loan to aid in acquiring Silverwoods, a 313-unit, 55+ multifamily community.
news.headline: Dwight Capital & Dwight Mortgage Trust secured over $364M in real estate financings in March 2025, including a $50M bridge loan.

news.summary: On April 9, 2025, in London, Paratus Holdings Limited ("Paratus"), which is known as the world's primary (re)insurance provider focused on underwriting energy price risk, successfully completed a commercial transaction with Low Carbon. news.headline: Paratus and Low Carbon partner to manage renewable energy price risk. World's first (re)insurance for energy.
